baseline.note: no legacy ledger — accounting started clean

reconciliation.scope_note: lifecycle counts are cumulative since canonical accounting began; baseline counts are legacy ledger totals that predate it; ledger/gate totals == baseline + accounted.

tradingview_latest_signal.indicator_name: Hermes BTC Pulse Composite v6 ProfitGate

BTCUSD.indicator_name: Hermes BTC Pulse Composite v6 ProfitGate

tradingview.note: TradingView alerts are candidate signals only — they cannot place, resize, or bypass trades; the strategy + execution gate remain the sole trade authority.

edge_vs_5min_outcome.note: observe-only: did the TradingView signal at entry predict the 5-min Chainlink outcome (signal_hit_rate vs baseline_up_rate), and did aligning help the bot win (aligned vs opposed bot_win_rate)? Never affects trading.

rsi_trend.note: observe-only: learns P(next 5-min outcome | RSI alert trend state) from the alert history and scores its own next-window predictions. Never trades.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

signal_gate.note: when active, a paper trade is taken only if a fresh TradingView signal agrees with the side; it can only PREVENT trades, never force or bypass them.

context_gate.note: hard prior gate: blocks proven-losing entry contexts (volume spikes, noise regime, far-from-resolution) before enough samples exist for the learned selectivity gate. Can only PREVENT trades — never forces, sizes, or bypasses the execution gate, which remains the sole trade authority.

down_bias_gate.note: restrict-only: harvest DOWN/TV asymmetry by blocking proven-losing UP stacks

mtf_gate.note: observe-only conflict veto: blocks 4m/5m fast-pair conflict only (loop arch default). require_confirm/side_align are opt-in restrict-only layers, not trade authority.

learning.note: the bot's own settled-trade experience (calibrated edge model) adjusts the directional probability; it grows as more trades settle. The execution-quality gate, paper-realism, and reconciliation are unchanged and still veto every trade — learning can never bypass them.

last_note.summary: With n=46 settled trades the bot shows positive EV (0.126 avg) and 63% win rate overall, driven by DOWN_STRONG (0.72 WR n=25), trending regimes (0.67 WR n=43), normal BB state (0.875 WR n=16), sell-pressure CVD, bearish-aligned MTF and dead-volume conditions; all other buckets with n<8 are ignored as noise. No material change possible versus prior (first analysis). Focus must stay on selectivity filters that already separate the 46 accepts from 31k rejects.
failing: expansion BB states (negative pnl n=16 combined)
>=2 or <=-2 z-score buckets (n<8, large negative pnl)
warnings: total sample still only 46; all conclusions low-power
observe-only mode means no live execution risk yet
many dimensions correlated so apparent edges may collapse together
changes_since_last: First analysis - no prior results to compare
focus_next: accumulate more samples in normal-BB + trending + DOWN_STRONG intersection
track calibration of signal strength vs realized EV
monitor whether neutral-candle and range-bottom edges survive n>=20

history.summary: With 44 settled trades, confirmed profitable patterns center on DOWN_STRONG signals (n=24, WR 0.75, +EV), trending regimes (n=41), normal BB state (n=15, WR 0.867), sell-pressure CVD, range-bottom/middle, and dead-volume conditions, all showing positive EV after costs with n>=8 and WR well above breakeven. UP signals and expansion BB states remain marginal or negative despite some EV positivity. Many buckets (e.g., z-score extremes, short TTC) are ignored due to n<8 and treated as noise.
changes_since_last: Initial analysis; no prior baseline available

history.summary: With n=44 settled trades the bot shows positive overall EV (0.1286) and 63.6% win-rate, driven by DOWN_STRONG (n=24, 75% WR), trending regimes (n=41), sell_pressure CVD (n=17), range_bottom and neutral candles; most buckets with n>=8 remain profitable after costs. Many sub-buckets still have n<8 or mixed results (expansion_up BB, range_top, active volume) and are treated as noise. Sample is still tiny so all patterns require continued observation only.

With 44 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, sell_pressure CVD, bearish MTF alignment, and range_bottom/middle show confirmed positive EV (win-rates 0.70-0.91, n>=10-24) while UP signals and mixed/expansion states remain marginal or negative. Sample size still small overall; most buckets with n>=8 are directionally consistent with edge but lack power for full confirmation. No material changes possible without prior_analysis.

DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes with normal BB state, range_bottom/middle, bearish_aligned MTF, sell_pressure CVD, and low-spread conditions show confirmed positive EV (win-rates 0.70-0.91 above breakeven, n>=10-24). UP signals and expansion BB states remain marginal or negative despite some EV positivity. Overall sample of 44 trades is still modest but growing selectivity evidence favors DOWN bias in dead-volume, strong-trend environments.

With 45 settled trades the signal shows confirmed positive EV (overall WR 0.644, avg EV after cost 0.127) concentrated in DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, normal BB, sell-pressure CVD, bearish-aligned MTF, range-bottom/middle and dead-volume regimes; all other buckets have n<8 or WR near or below breakeven and are ignored. DOWN direction and strong-signal levels remain the clearest edge while UP and weak signals are marginal. No material change from prior zero-sample state; selectivity remains extremely high (45/31711).

With 45 settled trades (n>=8 threshold applied), confirmed profitable patterns center on DOWN_STRONG signals (wr 0.75), trending regimes, normal BB state, sell_pressure CVD, bearish_aligned MTF, and 120-240s TTC, all showing positive EV after costs and win-rates above breakeven. Many buckets remain underpowered or mixed (e.g., active volume, expansion_up BB, mixed MTF) and appear as noise. Overall edge is positive but driven by a narrow set of conditions.
changes_since_last: first analysis, sample at 45 trades with positive aggregate EV

history.summary: With 45 settled trades, confirmed profitable patterns center on DOWN_STRONG signals (n=24, wr=0.75, +EV), trending Hurst regime (n=42, wr=0.69), z-score -2..1, 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, dead volume, bearish-aligned MTF, sell-pressure CVD, and range-bottom/middle states—all with n>=8, positive EV after costs, and wr comfortably above breakeven. Weak or failing buckets include >=240s TTC (wr=0.5), expansion_up BB (wr=0.375), mixed MTF, and active volume despite larger n. Sample remains small overall; no material change from prior (initial) analysis.
changes_since_last: Initial analysis; no prior results to compare

history.summary: With n=46 settled trades the bot shows positive EV (0.126 avg) and 63% win rate overall, driven by DOWN_STRONG (0.72 WR n=25), trending regimes (0.67 WR n=43), normal BB state (0.875 WR n=16), sell-pressure CVD, bearish-aligned MTF and dead-volume conditions; all other buckets with n<8 are ignored as noise. No material change possible versus prior (first analysis). Focus must stay on selectivity filters that already separate the 46 accepts from 31k rejects.
changes_since_last: First analysis - no prior results to compare

analyst_A.note: observe-only Grok research analyst that LEARNS the bot's trading patterns from its growing settled-trade/selectivity/late-window evidence and refines across cycles (continuity). Diagnostics only — never trades.

predictor_B.note: observe-only Grok P(up) per signal; graded vs realized BTC move before it could ever be trusted; never places/sizes/bypasses a trade.

grok_signal_intel.note: A analyzes signal-learning performance; B predicts P(up) per signal and is graded vs realized moves. Both observe-only — never place/size/bypass a trade; the execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

aggression.note: loosens (more explore/looser exploit/larger size) as acted trades profit; tightens on losses; circuit breaker is the hard floor.

grok_decider.note: Grok decides; bot executes (paper). shadow=decide+grade only (no trade), follow=engine follows direction/size subject to the deterministic floor (execution realism, risk caps, freshness). Fail-closed -> no_trade.

verifier.note: independent Claude maker-checker; can only veto/shrink a Grok-proposed paper trade, never force one; fail-open; PAPER ONLY.

loops.note: formalized sub-loops (data/signal/verify/execute/risk/research/news/lessons); each has a trigger + skill + verifier + verifiable stop + a liveness watchdog (last_beat_age_s/stalled). PAPER ONLY.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

cex_lead_edge.promotion_rule: n>=min AND wilson_lower(win_rate)>breakeven AND Brier_cex<Brier_market AND avg_pnl>0
cex_lead_edge.note: CEX-lead latency edge; benchmark is the MARKET price (already ~0.21 Brier). SHADOW grades only; GATED can PROPOSE a side on a proven bucket but the execution-quality gate + safety floor remain authoritative. PAPER ONLY.

arbitrage.note: risk-free dutch book: BUY-both (asks<$1) + SELL-both (mint $1 set, sell bids>$1); deterministic P&L; NEVER blended into directional. PAPER ONLY.

last_violations.description: 15m up-mid below nested 5m up-mid: P(up_15m) cannot be < P(up_5m) for overlapping window

dependency_arbitrage.note: LCMM nested-window scanner + optional paper execution on validated nested_implication violations.

arb_graph.note: Graph is rebuilt each tick from open windows; Grok edges are never tradable alone.

samples.note: Layer-2 observe-only unless execute path validates VWAP fills.

bregman_projection.note: Layer-2 observe-only; VWAP path remains authoritative.

baseline_cohort_gate.note: baseline quant path only: 180-240s TTC, high edge_score, strong CEX; UP also requires TV UP_STRONG

learned_selectivity_gate.note: rejects ONLY buckets confidently below their own breakeven win-rate (Wilson upper bound < breakeven AND net-negative PnL); coin-flip / not-significant buckets are NOT rejected. Can only make the bot MORE selective — never trades, resizes, or bypasses the execution gate. Exploration tracked separately.

bucket_evidence.rule: blocked iff confidently_losing AND fdr_significant (Wilson upper < breakeven, PF < min, BH-FDR q=0.10)

counterfactual.note: in-sample replay using final accumulated bucket evidence (diagnostic estimate)

gate.note: when enabled, only late-window AND high-conviction setups may trade; can only PREVENT trades, never force/size/bypass the execution gate.

edge_measurement.note: observe-only: grades whether late-window high-conviction trades win more (cohort vs other) from live settled trades; never affects trading.

arbitrage.note: arb halt only on anomaly (guaranteed_profit<=0); risk-free P&L is segregated

stop_conditions.note: quantitative kill switches checked each tick from settled ledger evidence; NOT the LLM's opinion. Halting blocks NEW entries only.

execution_realistic_edge.note: KL divergence is observe-only — large KL means model disagrees with market; never a buy signal when model Brier > market Brier.

arbitrage.note: risk-free dutch book: BUY-both (asks<$1) + SELL-both (mint $1 set, sell bids>$1); deterministic P&L; NEVER blended into directional. PAPER ONLY.

baseline.note: no legacy ledger — accounting started clean

reconciliation.scope_note: lifecycle counts are cumulative since canonical accounting began; baseline counts are legacy ledger totals that predate it; ledger/gate totals == baseline + accounted.

execution_realistic_edge.note: KL divergence is observe-only — large KL means model disagrees with market; never a buy signal when model Brier > market Brier.

selectivity_impact.note: in-sample replay using final accumulated bucket evidence (diagnostic estimate)

analyst_A.note: observe-only Grok research analyst that LEARNS the bot's trading patterns from its growing settled-trade/selectivity/late-window evidence and refines across cycles (continuity). Diagnostics only — never trades.

predictor_B.note: observe-only Grok P(up) per signal; graded vs realized BTC move before it could ever be trusted; never places/sizes/bypasses a trade.

grok_signal_intel.note: A analyzes signal-learning performance; B predicts P(up) per signal and is graded vs realized moves. Both observe-only — never place/size/bypass a trade; the execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

cex_lead_edge.promotion_rule: n>=min AND wilson_lower(win_rate)>breakeven AND Brier_cex<Brier_market AND avg_pnl>0
cex_lead_edge.note: CEX-lead latency edge; benchmark is the MARKET price (already ~0.21 Brier). SHADOW grades only; GATED can PROPOSE a side on a proven bucket but the execution-quality gate + safety floor remain authoritative. PAPER ONLY.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

trading_performance.note: Profitability, win-rate, side balance (DOWN>UP), and ledger integrity.

operation.note: Engine health: reconciliation, loops, stops, and pipeline activity.

external_signals.note: External signal quality and how well signals align with bot outcomes.
